qa_pairs.question: Why might a submitted job not produce output immediately, and how can I monitor its progress on the cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: A submitted job might not write output immediately due to aggressive buffering by the Slurm scheduler, which aggregates many output lines before flushing them to the log file. To monitor the progress of your submitted job as it runs, you should consider running an interactive job. This is also a good way to determine how much time your job needs.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information regarding job priority determination and managing job competition within a research group on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For a discussion on how job priority is determined and how factors like time limits may affect the scheduling of your jobs, you should consult the "Job scheduling policies" documentation. If jobs within your research group are competing with one another, please refer to the "Managing Slurm accounts" page.
